progress:
# GSD-T Progress

## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
## Status: M46 DEFINED — Unattended Iter-Parallel + Worker Fan-Out Completion · built in-session using M44 parallel+headless dispatch
## Date: 2026-04-23
## Version: 3.18.17

## Current Milestone

**M46 Unattended Iter-Parallel + Worker Fan-Out Completion** — DEFINED 2026-04-23.

**Goal**: Close the two biggest gaps from the five-surface parallelism audit (2026-04-23 14:16): (2A) unattended multi-iteration parallelism — the supervisor main loop at `bin/gsd-t-unattended.cjs:969` runs iterations strictly serially; (2B) worker-side sub-fan-out — a supervisor worker invoked via `GSD_T_UNATTENDED_WORKER=1` in `commands/gsd-t-resume.md` Step 0 bypasses reattach but never invokes `runDispatch` on its own. The net effect today: unattended parallelism is capped at the supervisor's single-iter fan-out (one `_spawnWorkerFanOut` call per iter) and sub-worker fan-out happens only when the worker incidentally drops into `execute`/`quick`. M46 ships iteration-level parallelism (Promise.all over mode-safe iter batches) AND a deterministic worker-side runDispatch hand-off, unlocking the full 5× throughput objective on the unattended leg.

**Why build in-session (not unattended)**: surface #1 (in-session parallel + headless) is VERIFIED production-ready (`bin/gsd-t-parallel.cjs:349` `runDispatch` + `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs::autoSpawnHeadless`, wired in `commands/gsd-t-{execute,quick,integrate,debug}.md`, observed live in `bin/m44-proof-measure.cjs` as `T_par/T_seq ≈ 0.251`, 3.98× speedup). Building M46 in-session leverages that machinery directly rather than trying to dogfood M46 through itself (chicken-and-egg). The user directive (2026-04-23 14:32) confirmed: "let build these insession with parallel and headless" + "in session properly handles parallel and headless now so it will build much faster."

**Two coupled deliverables (file-disjoint, parallel-eligible)**:

- **D1 — Iteration-level parallelism in the supervisor main loop**. Promote the serial `while (!isDone(state) && !stopCheck(projectDir))` at `bin/gsd-t-unattended.cjs:969` to a batched fan-out: each pass the planner (via `runParallel` at line 1030) produces up to N concurrent iteration slices; supervisor runs them via `Promise.all`, each slice being one `spawnWorker`/`_spawnWorkerFanOut` call with its own `state.iter` value. Preserves all existing hard constraints (heartbeat, wall-clock cap, iteration cap, destructive-action gate, state.json atomicity — each concurrent slice gets its own iter counter segment; writes are serialized through a mutex since state.json is a single file). Mode math: unattended contract caps per-worker CW at 60%; the new iter-parallel gate is `sum(estimated_cw_pct_i) ≤ N × 60%` where each `i` is independent (no shared context between iters by definition — each iter already spawns a fresh `claude -p`). If the estimator can't prove disjointness across iter slices, fall back to serial (exactly today's behavior). Files owned: `bin/gsd-t-unattended.cjs` (main loop refactor + new helpers), new `test/m46-d1-iter-parallel.test.js`, new contract `.gsd-t/contracts/iter-parallel-contract.md` v1.0.0.

- **D2 — Worker-side runDispatch in the worker resume path**. When `GSD_T_UNATTENDED_WORKER=1` is set, `commands/gsd-t-resume.md` Step 0 currently bypasses the supervisor-reattach check but then falls through to the normal resume (which only invokes parallel dispatch if the next action is `execute`/`quick`). D2 adds a deterministic node-shim check after the bypass: read the worker's target task graph (via `bin/gsd-t-task-graph.cjs`), ask `runParallel --dry-run --mode unattended`, and if it returns `workerCount ≥ 2` with disjointness=true, invoke `runDispatch` directly — hand off to N sub-workers in place of the single serial pass. If the planner says `workerCount == 1` (no subtasks, or disjointness fails), fall through to the existing worker flow. Files owned: `commands/gsd-t-resume.md` (new bash block inside the `GSD_T_UNATTENDED_WORKER=1` branch — additive, does not touch the bypass prose), new `bin/gsd-t-worker-dispatch.cjs` (deterministic probe helper so the command file doesn't inline logic), new `test/m46-d2-worker-subdispatch.test.js`, new contract clause in `.gsd-t/contracts/headless-default-contract.md` (v2.0.0 → v2.1.0 additive, documenting the worker-side sub-dispatch invariant).
lastDecisionLogEntry: - 2026-04-23: [quick · v3.18.15] Supervisor false-failed-marker fix — root cause identified during M45 close-out: `bin/headless-exit-codes.cjs::mapHeadlessExitCode` used free-form substring matchers (`lower.includes("tests failed")`, `"verification failed"`, `"context budget exceeded"`) that fired on healthy worker narration. M45 worker output contained `"tests failed"` 6× in prose (`"0 tests failed"`, quoted examples, documentation phrases) → mapped clean exit 0 → 1 → supervisor finalized `status=failed` despite the milestone being completed and archived. **Fix**: (1) replaced the four substring matchers with polarity-sound regexes — `NONZERO_FAILURE_COUNT_RE` requires `[1-9]\d*` count prefix, `STRUCTURED_FAIL_RE` requires `^FAIL[:\s]`, `JEST_SUMMARY_FAIL_RE` requires `^Tests:\s+\d+\s+failed`, `VERIFICATION_FAILED_RE` / `CONTEXT_BUDGET_RE` require start-of-line or post-sentence-boundary anchor; blocker compound unchanged (already polarity-sound via `\b`). (2) `commands/gsd-t-unattended-watch.md` Step 3 reconciliation: when PID absent + `state.status=failed` + fresh milestone archive exists (mtime ≥ `state.startedAt`), render reconciled success report treating archive as authority; raw report preserved otherwise. Audit of `bin/gsd-t-unattended-safety.cjs::detectGutter` / `detectBlockerSentinel` found existing matchers are already structurally bounded (iteration-block-scoped + `\b`-anchored compound phrases) — no change needed. **Tests**: new `test/m45-fix-headless-exit-polarity.test.js` 27/27 pass (narration-must-not-fire + genuine-failure-still-fires + existing-contract-case-compatibility). Existing `test/headless.test.js` 41/41 preserved. **Doc ripple**: CHANGELOG v3.18.15 "### Fixed — Supervisor false-failed marker", `package.json` 3.18.14 → 3.18.15.
